Business Agent l.. L. Quinn of the
trades council was the first witness
called. He gave n history of the
strike from its inception and was
cross examined as to details by all
the members of the board, incl Ming
Commissioner Blackman.


The session was open to the public
and many labor leaders as well as all
the officials OF the gas company were
present.


State Labor Commissioner Black
man. in pursuance Of his duty under
the law, at the request of the mill
workers ON strike. called upon Presi-
dent Van Dissel of the millmens as
ISOLATION this nSrning in an effort
TO adjust the differences existing be-
tween its members and their men.


President Van Dissel stated the
matter of arbitration would be taken
under consideration and an early an
swer is expected from the association
